title: [ValueTracking] Propagate sign information out of loop
author: kshitijvp
description:
LLVM converts sqrt libcall to intrinsic call if the argument is within the range(greater than or equal to 0.0). In this case the compiler is not able to deduce the non-negativity on its own. Extended ValueTracking to understand such loops.

Fixes llvm/llvm-project#174813

target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define double @CompareDistmats(double noundef %distmat1_, double noundef %distmat2_) {
entry:
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %RMSD.0 = phi double [ 0.000000e+00, %entry ], [ %fmacall, %for.body ]
  %cmp = phi i1 [ true, %entry ], [ false, %for.body ]
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  %call = call double @sqrt(double noundef %RMSD.0)
  ret double %call

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %sub = fsub double %distmat1_, %distmat2_
  %fmacall = call double @llvm.fmuladd.f64(double noundef %sub, double noundef %sub, double %RMSD.0)
  br label %for.cond
}

@kshitijvp you might want to use this testcase instead: https://godbolt.org/z/shWv73qbj

The current one will not work because you cannot prove isGuaranteedNotToBeUndef on X (based on the discussion here: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/175166)

```
define dso_local noundef double @CompareDistmats(double, double)(double noundef %distmat1_, double noundef %distmat2_) local_unnamed_addr {
entry:
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %RMSD.0 = phi double [ 0.000000e+00, %entry ], [ %0, %for.body ]
  %cmp = phi i1 [ true, %entry ], [ false, %for.body ]
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  %call = call double @sqrt(double noundef %RMSD.0)
  ret double %call

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %sub = fsub double %distmat1_, %distmat2_
  %0 = call double @llvm.fmuladd.f64(double %sub, double %sub, double %RMSD.0)
  br label %for.cond
}
```
